root_cause: Deployment rollout stalled while transitioning to a new ReplicaSet
evidence: Deployment 'web' observed generation 12/12 but only 2/4 replicas are available
Recent controller rollout activity spans 17.0 minutes across 4 events, exceeding the 10.0 minute progress deadline
Older ReplicaSets still hold 3 replicas and 2 available replicas, so the rollout has not converged

causes.message: Deployment 'web' has been observed at generation 12
The Deployment controller is no longer making rollout progress toward the desired replica set state
New ReplicaSet 'web-7f9f6c86d5' is scaled but not becoming fully available
Deployment availability remains below target (2/4) while the rollout is incomplete

replicaset:web-6c9d8f7b5c: Older rollout revision still active with 3 replicas

likely_causes: New revision pods are failing readiness, startup, or container runtime checks so the new ReplicaSet never becomes available
Scheduling or cluster capacity constraints are slowing the replacement pods enough to exhaust the Deployment progress deadline
Older pods cannot drain or terminate cleanly because of PodDisruptionBudget, long shutdown time, or stuck finalization
ReplicaSet or admission-level errors are preventing the Deployment controller from completing the intended scale transition
